note: NODE-BOUNDARY parity: the unit of truth is ONE .motr node + its params -> ONE isolated computation, verified faithful (TS port == REAL FCP) across the node's param space. NOT a hand-picked C++ symbol, NOT whole-frame PSNR through the full pipeline. See DESIGN.md. kinds: filter(image->image, isolate via filter_probe.py+_filter_apply.ts), curve(value->value, the computation a <curve interp=N> segment triggers — the exact interpolation, verified bit-exact via the dlsym oracle), transform(params->matrix), generator(params->image). status: UNTESTED|VERIFIED|DIVERGED|NO_SIGNAL|NO_ORACLE.

filter.note: FAITHFUL delta-response ddb in the node's real host (delegated to fct/faithful). Static-source injection is unfaithful (synth.py lesson). Signal-gated.

generator.note: FAITHFUL delta-response ddb in the node's real host (delegated to fct/faithful). Static-source injection is unfaithful (synth.py lesson). Signal-gated.

transfer.note: EXACT per-pixel transfer via uniform-colour inputs (conform-invariant). VERIFIED when max abs channel error <= tol_levels (quantisation). Sidesteps pipeline coupling + headless-vs-GUI for pointwise colour nodes.

spatial.note: FULL-FRAME node-boundary PSNR: one injected spatial filter over a native-canvas synthetic input through REAL headless FCP vs the TS filter (_filter_apply). The SPATIAL analog of the transfer probe — for kernel filters whose neighbourhood dependence a center-pixel read can't capture. VERIFIED when worst-case full-frame RGB PSNR >= pass_db. Closes the gap left by the faithful delta-response, which only measures a spatial node's PARAM RESPONSE inside its real (often multi-stage) host.

nodes.desc: Keyframe segment interp=15/16/17 (Ease/Accelerate/Decelerate): value shaped by PCMath::easeInOut over normalized time.
Stored-tangent bezier segment value evaluation (OZBezierEval): the value-domain cubic the engine's cubicBezier ports.
Bezier time reparameterization (OZBezierFindParameter): invert the time-bezier for u, the engine's solveBezierParam.
HGBlur::GetDecimation(radius): the blur decimation-level choice (large blur -> decimate 2^level, blur small, upsample). Exact integer function the engine ports as gaussianDecimation.

dSFMT (MEXP=19937) close1_open2 RNG — the deterministic Stage-1 white-noise generator behind PAENoise + PAECloudsV. Engine's bit-exact TS DSFMT port (noise.ts) vs FCP's real ProCore RandMersenne (SetSeed + dsfmt_gen_rand_all via dlsym). Proves the seeded byte sequence is bit-for-bit identical to Apple's; the per-FRAME reseed schedule remains host-side/unrecoverable (separate concern).

Rec.709 luminance coefficients — engine LUMA709_COEFFS_FCP (blend.ts) vs FCP's own ProCore PCGetGamutColorSpaceLuminanceCoefficients(0). EXACT vector match; independently confirms the Rec.709 luma used across the decoded colour subsystem (Tint/HSV/Colorize) is FCP's authoritative working-space luma.

Colour working-space GAMMA — engine's magic constant (iv=0.51117 -> gamma 1.9563, used by Levels/Contrast/HSV/ChannelMixer WS paths) vs FCP's OWN PCEstimateGamma(PCGetWorkingColorSpaceGammaEquivalent(Rec709)) = 1.9609375. Turns the fitted magic number into a function-VERIFIED constant. They agree within 8-bit quantisation (0.005 gamma delta -> <=0.3 code levels on every colour transfer), confirming the fitted WS gamma is faithful to FCP's authoritative value.
nodes.const_note: engine iv=0.51117; FCP Rec709 working gamma=1.9609375; delta 0.0046 (sub-quantisation)

nodes.desc: Rec.709 sRGB->YCbCr matrix — the HSV-hue rotation basis (decoded 2026-07-23: FCP's HSV Hue is a Cb/Cr chroma-plane rotation in the gamma-1.958 working space). Verifies the engine's hardcoded RGB2YCBCR (hue-saturation.ts) against FCP's OWN stored matrix HGColorMatrix::sRGBtoYCbCr, read bit-for-bit from Helium __TEXT,__const. Turns the engine's matrix from a hardcoded triple into a binary-VERIFIED constant.
nodes.const_note: engine RGB2YCBCR vs FCP HGColorMatrix::sRGBtoYCbCr (Helium binary); agree within ~3.2e-5 (sub-8-bit-quantisation). Rec.709 luma row (0.2126,0.7152,0.0722).

nodes.desc: PAEBrightness combined. Darken+gray VERIFIED (see _darken). Brighten on saturated colours (amount>1) DIVERGES via the HGColorMatrix over-1.0 clamp (highlight-rolloff, needs GPU disasm — evidence/hgcolormatrix_clamp_probe.json). [RESOLVED 2026-07-23: the over-1.0 'clamp' was a CoreGraphics ExtendedLinearSRGB->sRGB readback artifact in the shim (NOT a GPU op); this node is now VERIFIED against the clean OZ_CLAMP_UNIT oracle — the engine's per-channel effect is faithful.]
nodes.characterized_reason: Cross-channel HGColorMatrix in a non-sRGB working space (see report). Isolated headless transfer != GUI-gate truth; closing needs a linear-working-space filter CHAIN (encode once), an engine-arch change tracked separately. Per-channel multiply shipped intentionally (GUI GT prefers it for the stacked chain).

nodes.desc: PAEBrightness darken leg (amount<=1) + gray: EXACT per-channel code-space multiply out=clip(v*amount). VERIFIED 2026-07-22 vs REAL FCP. The brighten leg on saturated colours (amount>1) is the separate HGColorMatrix over-1.0 clamp — see transfer.PAEBrightness (CHARACTERIZED).
nodes.characterized_reason: Cross-channel HGColorMatrix in a non-sRGB working space (see report). Isolated headless transfer != GUI-gate truth; closing needs a linear-working-space filter CHAIN (encode once), an engine-arch change tracked separately. Per-channel multiply shipped intentionally (GUI GT prefers it for the stacked chain).

nodes.desc: PAEBrightness BRIGHTEN regime (gain>1) on NEUTRAL/low-saturation inputs that clamp uniformly (no cross-channel over-1.0 redistribution). Complements Brightness_darken (gain<=1). The saturated-primary brighten cases stay CHARACTERIZED in the parent transfer.PAEBrightness (shared HGColorMatrix clamp).
PAEHSVAdjust combined (Hue+Saturation+Value). Value+Saturation VERIFIED in gamma-1.958 WS (see _valsat); Hue leg still CHARACTERIZED (see _hue). Combined stays DIVERGED until Hue decoded.
nodes.characterized_reason: Hue UNIT bug FIXED (degrees->radians, commit d5840c2, gate-neutral). Residual 107 levels is the linear-working-space sat/value coupling (FCP does HSV sat/value in linear light) — same class as PAEBrightness's cross-channel/working-space issue, gate-blocked (a per-filter linear encode regresses the GUI gate; needs the chain-level linear pipeline). Not an open unit bug.

nodes.desc: PAEHSVAdjust Value+Saturation legs in the DECODED gamma-1.958 working space (Rec.709 luma; Value = ws-linear multiply, Saturation = ws lerp to luma). VERIFIED 2026-07-22 vs REAL FCP (Value/Sat sub-legs).
nodes.characterized_reason: Hue UNIT bug FIXED (degrees->radians, commit d5840c2, gate-neutral). Residual 107 levels is the linear-working-space sat/value coupling (FCP does HSV sat/value in linear light) — same class as PAEBrightness's cross-channel/working-space issue, gate-blocked (a per-filter linear encode regresses the GUI gate; needs the chain-level linear pipeline). Not an open unit bug.

nodes.desc: PAEHSVAdjust Saturation leg (WS luma-lerp; satFactor=max(0,1+Sat)). Covers the DESATURATION range incl. the LOWER CLAMP: FCP clamps satFactor at 0, so Sat<=-1 all give full gray (decoded 2026-07-23). Saturated inputs (desaturation stays in-gamut). Oversaturation (Sat>0) hits the shared over-1.0 clamp, tested elsewhere.

PAEHSVAdjust Value = linear MULTIPLY in the gamma-1.958 working space (neutral=1.0, Value=0->black, decoded S0V0 probe). Covers the multiplier range on NEUTRAL inputs where the WS multiply is exact (uniform clip, no cross-channel coupling; saturated over-clip is the shared HGColorMatrix clamp, tested separately). Complements valsat's 2 Value points.

PAEHSVAdjust Hue-rotation leg. CHARACTERIZED: FCP's hue rotation is NOT a plain HSV-hextant rotation (S/V not preserved: V drops, S rises), NOR a standard NTSC/YIQ/Rodrigues chroma rotation (all fit >26 rms). Needs the exact HgcHSVAdjust hue reconstruction decoded. GATE-NEUTRAL: all 4 shipping HSV hosts author Hue=0.
nodes.characterized_reason: Hue UNIT bug FIXED (degrees->radians, commit d5840c2, gate-neutral). Residual 107 levels is the linear-working-space sat/value coupling (FCP does HSV sat/value in linear light) — same class as PAEBrightness's cross-channel/working-space issue, gate-blocked (a per-filter linear encode regresses the GUI gate; needs the chain-level linear pipeline). Not an open unit bug.

nodes.desc: PAEHSVAdjust Hue rotation, IN-GAMUT small-to-moderate regime (6-30 deg). DECODED+VERIFIED 2026-07-23: the luma-preserving Rec.709-YCbCr hue rotation matches REAL headless FCP within 1 lvl for rotations up to ~30 deg on both in-gamut and saturated colours (the previously-DIVERGED transfer.PAEHSVAdjust_hue hit 107 lvl only at 90 deg, where colours rotate far out of gamut into the shared HGColorMatrix clamp). Split mirrors Contrast/Brightness in-gamut nodes. Positive hue only (negative hue is the decoded no-op clamp).

PAETint per-pixel transfer (HgcTint hard-light in gamma-1.956 working space, Rec.709 luma, tint decoded from ~sRGB). DECODED+VERIFIED 2026-07-22. Nested Color group. EXACT isolation via uniform inputs.
nodes.characterized_reason: DECODED (2026-07-22, near-complete — see evidence/tint_transfer_r1g0b0_i1.json FULL_TWO_LEG_DECODE): highlight leg = K*luma_sRGB*s2l(tint) K=3.878 (fits ~0.1 lvl, tint colour LINEARIZED); shadow leg ~2*luma-1 smoothstepped near 0.5; Intensity mix ~sRGB. The documented ceiling is cracked structurally. REMAINING to ship: pin K exactly (read SetParameter scale in disasm) + the smoothstep edges, implement in tintFilter + nested-Color read, then verify Objects__Leaves >=22.44 on the GUI gate (the compound fix, now with decoded math).

nodes.desc: PAEColorize per-pixel luma remap: out = mix(in, mix(Black,White,luma), Amount). EXACT isolation via uniform inputs.
nodes.characterized_reason: Luma-remap matches FCP at black=[0,0,0] but diverges with a nonzero black point (working-space mix, not sRGB). Same gate-blocked working-space root cause as Brightness/Tint + the faithful Colorize note (s2l endpoints match headless but regress the GUI gate).

nodes.desc: PAELevels GAMMA transfer (Histogram>RGB>Gamma) on grayscale. Per-channel power curve — faithful in sRGB.
PAEChannelMixer per-pixel 3x3 matrix multiply. EXACT isolation via uniform inputs — tests whether the channel dot is done in sRGB (engine) or linear (FCP).
PAELevels Black/White In REMAP (per-channel linear stretch toward endpoints). Tests whether the endpoint remap is faithful in sRGB (like ChannelMixer) or needs linear space (like Colorize).
nodes.characterized_reason: Black/White In endpoint remap diverges (working-space); FCP stretches gentler than TS sRGB. Same gate-blocked linear-working-space root cause as Colorize/Brightness. Gamma (separate node) stays VERIFIED — only endpoint-relative remaps diverge.

nodes.desc: ChannelMixer with amplifying diagonal (Red-Red=2 etc) = same as Brightness x2 via the SAME HGColorMatrix primitive, but through ChannelMixer. Tests whether HGColorMatrix CLAMP couples cross-channel.
nodes.characterized_reason: Diagonal-1.5x ChannelMixer == Brightness x1.5 bit-identical in FCP — confirms Brightness=HGColorMatrix + unifies the divergence to the shared HGColorMatrix over-1.0 CLAMP (lifts non-clipped channels). Non-clip matrices VERIFIED exact. Decode = HGColorMatrix render disasm; gate-blocked.

nodes.desc: PAEThreshold per-pixel transfer: luma (0.3086/0.6094/0.082 — NOT Rec.709) -> smoothstep(threshold, smoothness) -> lerp(Dark,Light). Pointwise; EXACT isolation via uniform inputs. Feeds Bloom/Glow threshold masks.

PAEContrast per-pixel transfer: affine scale about a 0.5 pivot in the gamma-1.958 working space (out_ws = 0.5 + (ws(in)-0.5)*Contrast). DECODED+VERIFIED 2026-07-22 (0.18 rms). Same unified colour WS as Tint/HSV/Colorize/Levels. 12+ shipping hosts.

PAEContrast IN-GAMUT regime (contrast c<=1.25) across the full swatch set incl. saturated primaries. Isolates the VERIFIED gamma-1.958 WS affine-about-0.5-pivot contrast (matches FCP on gray AND saturated colours until a channel exceeds 1.0) from the CHARACTERIZED c=1.5/2.0-on-saturated cases that hit the shared HGColorMatrix over-1.0 clamp. Mirrors the Brightness_darken / HSVAdjust_combined_ingamut splits.

PAEContrast on GRAY/neutral inputs — the pointwise decode (affine about 0.5 in gamma-1.958 WS) VERIFIED exact (0.18 rms). Saturated-colour over-clip is the shared HGColorMatrix GPU-readback lift, isolated in transfer.PAEContrast (characterized).

PAELevels output remap (Black Out/White Out): affine out=ws_inv(bo+ws(in)*(wo-bo)) in the gamma-1.958 WS, raw endpoints. DECODED+param-IDs VERIFIED 2026-07-22 (Black Out=2, White Out=4). Same unified colour WS.
PAEFill (Color mode): out = lerp(input, fillColor, Mix). Pointwise; EXACT isolation via uniform inputs. Checks the fill-lerp space (code vs working) and nested Color IDs.

PAELevels ALL stages together (in-remap + gamma + out-remap). CHARACTERIZED 2026-07-22: the stages do NOT naively compose in one WS pass. DECODED order = in-remap+gamma in the gamma-1.958 WS, DECODE to code, then output-remap in CODE space (out=code*(wo-bo)+bo*255). This brings the combined error 12->3.4 rms; a ~3-level mid offset + ~12 at the white-in clip edge remain (clip-interaction, further decode). Each leg VERIFIED alone (Levels/_remap/_outremap).

PAELevels PER-CHANNEL histogram (Histogram > Red/Green/Blue groups, ids 3/4/5). FCP applies the same WS HgcLevels transfer to an individual channel (master RGB=id2 composes first). The engine previously read ONLY the RGB master and ignored per-channel groups (no-op); levelsFilterPerChannel now composes master + per-channel. env FCT_LEVELS_WS.

PAEChannelMixer output OFFSET (the 'Red - Alpha' constant column): out_ch += offset*255 in code space. DECODED 2026-07-22 (delta exactly offset*255). Resolves the prior 'non-converged offset' gap — that was the shared over-1.0 clamp on clipping inputs, not the offset op. Gray/non-clip inputs only.

PAEHSVAdjust with Hue+Saturation+Value combined. VERIFIES the WS-HSV legs COMPOSE: in-gamut cases match ~0.4 lvl (c1 desat+dim, c3 hue+desat+dim). Over-saturation+value>1 on saturated inputs diverges via the shared HGColorMatrix over-1.0 clamp (characterized). [RESOLVED 2026-07-23: the over-1.0 'clamp' was a CoreGraphics ExtendedLinearSRGB->sRGB readback artifact in the shim (NOT a GPU op); this node is now VERIFIED against the clean OZ_CLAMP_UNIT oracle — the engine's per-channel effect is faithful.]

PAEHSVAdjust multi-param composition, IN-GAMUT regime (dim/desaturate — no over-1.0 highlight). Isolates the VERIFIED HSV leg composition (Hue+Sat+Value compose exactly in the gamma-1.958 working space) from the CHARACTERIZED oversaturate_bright case, which hits the shared HGColorMatrix over-1.0 clamp. Mirrors the Brightness_darken/Brightness split.
PAEColorize node (filter). Verified via the FAITHFUL delta-response in its real host (delegated to fct/faithful).
nodes.characterized_reason: Delegated in-host faithful-delta node DIVERGES because the host transition's geometry/composite pipeline entangles it (the documented delegation artifact: e.g. Glow reads 13.5 dB inside the Bloom host but 40.4 dB at its own node boundary). The node's COMPUTATION is decoded + VERIFIED at its NODE BOUNDARY via transfer.PAEColorize (VERIFIED 0.91 at node boundary). The transfer/spatial regimes supersede delegation for this node; the delegated metric is retained only as a host-integration signal, not a node-correctness verdict.

nodes.badtv_decode_note: 2026-07-23: waviness=verified dSFMT(seed=2*frame+1) cumulative walk (per-row validated ~0.5px); scan-line band polarity FIXED (bright=*ScanBright, dark=identity, validated ~1lvl on flat inputs) — a real gate-invisible bug. Remaining DIVERGED cause: scan-line brightness is a FRAME-PEAK AGC (a 2%-area white square lifts the whole dark field 61->133), which the per-pixel model cannot capture; + the Static overlay is the separate mt19937 2-D white field. Both need frame-statistic decode (deep). The per-pixel deterministic path (waviness+scanline+saturate) is decoded+wired behind FCT_BADTV_WAVINESS_EXACT.

nodes.desc: PAECloudsV node (scenenode). Verified via the FAITHFUL delta-response in its real host (delegated to fct/faithful).
nodes.decode_note: CloudsV2 noise ALGORITHM fully decoded + unit-tested (engine clouds.ts: makeCloudsPermTable + CLOUDS_GRADIENTS + cloudsNoise2D; 7 tests incl. the definitive integer-lattice-zero Perlin property). It is STANDARD 2D Perlin gradient noise with a DETERMINISTIC seeded generator (same LCG+Bays-Durham as Underwater), NOT the "unrecoverable per-frame sequence" the older note claimed. NODE-BOUNDARY PSNR verification is EXTERNALLY BLOCKED: (1) rendering PAECloudsV2 solo through headless FCP SEGFAULTS (confirmed 2026-07-23 — the documented FreqSynth-texture-not-bound headless instability), and (2) the synth scaffold composites generators as black at t=0/0.5 in a standalone render (the faithful driver measures a param-DELTA, not a solo frame). Banking a spatial.PAECloudsV needs a generator-solo harness that survives the headless segfault. Decode DONE; verification gated on FCP headless stability.

nodes.desc: PAEEarthquake node (filter). Verified via the FAITHFUL delta-response in its real host (delegated to fct/faithful).
nodes.superseded_reason: Node-boundary RNG+translation DECODED EXACT (spatial.PAEEarthquake: LCG+Bays-Durham shuffle bit-verified, disc |shift|=144.00 vs predicted 143.84px, h8=46.6/v8=50.6 dB at t=0). The delegated in-host faithful_delta (ddb 16.3) conflates the per-frame shake pose with the host transition's animated Twist/Shake curves + t!=0 frame-index mapping (host-internal secs*fps*1000), which the isolated boundary probe removes by rendering at t=0. Same delegation artifact as Glow-in-Bloom.
nodes.supersede_reason: Node-boundary decode VERIFIED at t=0 by spatial.PAEEarthquake: the LCG+Bays-Durham RNG is bit-exact (disc centroid shift 144.00 vs predicted 143.84px) and translation cases read 46-51 dB. The delegated in-host faithful-delta (16.3 ddb) conflates the per-FRAME re-randomized shake (whose t!=0 host frame-index/fps mapping is host-internal) with the transition's own animation; at the node boundary with the host time ambiguity removed (t=0) the computation is exact. Only the pure-rotation resample is a documented bilinear ceiling (38.6 dB), not a decode error.

nodes.desc: PAEGlow at its own node boundary: full-frame PSNR of the isolated single-filter render (mask ramp + decimated Gaussian blur + HgcGlowCombineFx) vs REAL headless FCP at native 1920x1080. filter.PAEGlow delegates to the faithful delta INSIDE Lights__Bloom and reads 13.5 dB (a Bloom-pipeline artifact); isolated here PAEGlow matches at 40+ dB, proving the decode is faithful and the divergence lives in the Bloom host, not the Glow node.

nodes.desc: PAEUnderwater node (filter). Verified via the FAITHFUL delta-response in its real host (delegated to fct/faithful).
nodes.superseded_reason: Node-boundary DECODED + verified: spatial.PAEUnderwater reads 30.9/29.8 dB at t=0 (exact-RNG wave field + corner-lerp displacement + affine host-M U,V; flow residual at the ~0.5px measurement floor). The delegated in-host faithful_delta (ddb 16.5) conflates the refraction with the host Movements/Flashback transition + the t!=0 animated phase (FreqSynth noise-texture unbound headless past t~0.5). Same delegation artifact as Glow-in-Bloom / Earthquake. Exact path env-gated (FCT_UNDERWATER_EXACT).

nodes.desc: PAEZoomBlur node (filter). Verified via the FAITHFUL delta-response in its real host (delegated to fct/faithful).
nodes.superseded_by: spatial.PAEZoomBlur (CHARACTERIZED log-polar ceiling at node boundary)
nodes.characterized_reason: Delegated in-host faithful-delta node. Its NODE-BOUNDARY computation is decoded+VERIFIED via spatial.PAEZoomBlur (CHARACTERIZED log-polar ceiling at node boundary). The delegated-host delta-response DIVERGES because it conflates this node with the host transition's geometry/composite (the documented host-pipeline artifact — cf. Glow reads 13.5 dB inside the Bloom host but 40.4 dB at its own node boundary). The node-boundary regime SUPERSEDES delegation for this node; the delegated metric is retained only as a host-integration signal, not a node-correctness verdict.

nodes.desc: PAEZoomBlur (spatial: radial zoom blur). Delegated faithful delta reads 15.8 dB inside its host; isolate at the node boundary.
nodes.characterized_reason: Log-polar zoom model verified 24-29 dB on rings but the off-centre hard-disc synth is a worst case (14.5 dB): FCP spreads the bright disc radially far more than the TS log-polar Gaussian at matched Amount (signed +12 inner / -21 mid). Residual is the documented log-polar centre-resampling ceiling (directional-blur.ts PHASE-2 CEILING NOTE); shipping users author Amount=0 (static) so gate-inert. Needs a super-sampled/centre-refined log-polar buffer.
nodes.note: log-polar zoom verified 24-29 dB on rings (directional-blur.ts L363); a hard off-center disc is a worst case. A large NEGATIVE signed bias (TS darker) means the TS log-polar spread is TOO WEAK vs FCP's radial energy spread — the real decode lead (ZOOM_LOG_K / Amount->sigma), separate from the resample ceiling.

nodes.desc: PAEGaussianBlur (spatial: decimated Gaussian). Absolute full-frame node-boundary check to complement the delta-response VERIFIED.

PAEDirectionalBlur (spatial: 1D directional blur). Absolute full-frame node-boundary check. Uses the TEXTURED synth (a flat frame gives a 1-D blur no signal). pass_db=24 reflects the documented polar-resample discretization ceiling (~24-26 dB, directional-blur.ts line 206) — a harness limit, not a decode error; signed bias ~0.
nodes.note: polar-resample discretization caps the all-edges ceiling ~24-26 dB (directional-blur.ts L206); a faithful decode is harness-limited here, not divergent — near-zero signed bias confirms the kernel math matches.

nodes.desc: PAEBloom at its node boundary: decode-faithful FLOAT bloom (HgcBloomThreshold extract x10-Thr/10 -> float blur 0.5*Amount -> HgcEchoScaleAndAdd additive orig+blur*(|Bright-50|*4/50), clip-to-white). Delegated filter.PAEBloom reads 7.0 dB inside its host; the notes report the float path matches headless ~50 dB isolated at shipping params vs the broken 8-bit glowFilter at 3.7 dB. engine_env selects the decoded float path (shipped default is still 8-bit, gated on host-composition work).
nodes.characterized_reason: Pointwise extract+combine LAW verified EXACT vs headless FCP across the full (input v in {60,97,128,190,255}) x (Threshold in {0,15,30,50,75,100}) grid at max |dR|=0.3 levels (HgcBloomThreshold x10-Thr/10 extract + HgcEchoScaleAndAdd additive orig+blur*(|Bright-50|*4/50), clip-to-white — all register-decoded). At the SHIPPING regime (Threshold=0, full-frame bloom to white) the spatial render VERIFIES at 42.7 dB. The Threshold>0 spatial divergence (27-30 dB) is NOT the decode: the residual is a uniform ~-1.5 level flat-field deficit (99.4% of pixels; edges match at MAD 0.5) = the shared decimated FLOAT blur spreads the sparse high-contrast extracted-highlight layer with a slightly lighter tail than FCP's Gaussian (disc energy: FCP reaches +7.6 more just outside the disc and +1.8 more in the far field). Same blur-tail ceiling family as Glow's dark-tail and Zoom's log-polar spread; retuning the shared sigma/truncation risks the VERIFIED PAEGaussianBlur (49 dB), so decode-don't-fit holds. Gate-inert: shipping Bloom slugs author Threshold->0 with full-frame bloom, the VERIFIED regime. Float path guarded by FCT_BLOOM_FLOAT (shipped default still 8-bit, gated on 360deg_Bloom host composition). LSF/ESF RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-23: a direct impulse (bright-column) + edge-spread (erf-fit) probe of PAEGaussianBlur at the node boundary proves FCP's Gaussian is a CLEAN 3-sigma-truncated Gaussian (actual codes = 0 at +3sigma, gaussian_pred 0.07-0.14; erf sigma=radius/6.13-6.28 confirming the engine radius/6.10). So Bloom's far-field deficit is NOT kernel tail-truncation and NOT a sigma error — the shared blur is faithful. The residual is a float decimate/upsample energy-distribution subtlety; retuning risks the VERIFIED PAEGaussianBlur (49 dB). Decode-don't-fit holds, now measurement-backed not assumed.

nodes.desc: PAELumaKeyer (HgcLumaKeyer, UUID 7E9178C5-…) default keyer blob: the keyed ALPHA is a LINEAR band-pass TRAPEZOID in the gamma-1.958 WORKING SPACE (xw=luma^0.51117), control points B=1/4 C=3/4 D=1. DECODED 2026-07-23 from a fine headless-FCP alpha ramp; the engine previously used a code-space SMOOTHSTEP rising edge (rms 12.82 / max 71 lvl WRONG in alpha — gate-invisible, RGB-only PSNR gate never checks alpha). RGB passes through unchanged; only alpha is keyed. Grays give a clean luma ramp.

PAEFlop (axis-aligned +/-1 mirror about the image center via Helium XForm; NO shader). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Flop in {0:H,1:V,2:Both}. Textured synth (asymmetric diagonal stripes) so the mirror is actually exercised (a symmetric smooth synth would give a false-high PSNR). Lossless pixel permutation for the integer-center case.

PAEPixellate (HgcPixellate coordinate-quantize: snap each output pixel to the centre of a Scale x Scale grid cell, nearest-sample). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Scale in {8,20,50}. Textured synth so the mosaic is exercised. Deterministic, no shipping transition uses it (byte-neutral to GUI gate).

PAEVignette (HgcVignette radial smoothstep darkening; fully decoded shader + oracle-verified band model R0=1.5-Size, inner/outer from Size*Falloff). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Size/Darken/Falloff. Smooth synth (radial darkening shows on the disc+gradient without texture aliasing).

PAEMinMax (Helium HGMinMax separable morphological erode/dilate: X-pass then Y-pass, full [-R,+R] (2R+1) window, integer pixel offsets, clamp-at-edge, on premult RGBA). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Mode in {0:Min/erode,1:Max/dilate} x Radius. Textured synth. Used by Dissolves/Divide (shipping).

PAEFisheye barrel/pincushion warp (exponent=Amount/30<=0 ? 1/(1-Amount/30) : Amount/30+1; per-axis anisotropic src_d = d*|d|_norm^(exponent-1), bilinear resample, BORDER/zero address mode for out-of-bounds -> black borders in the pincushion regime). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over positive (barrel) AND negative (pincushion) Amount. Textured synth. Amount 0 = identity. Decoded 2026-07-23: negative branch exponent matches 1/(1-a) (rms 1.2px on a line-grid probe); the pincushion divergence was the CLAMP-vs-BORDER address mode.

PAEScrape/Smear (HgcScrape verbatim inverse-map directional smear: for pixels past a threshold along the rotated axis, displace source sampling by (0.5*thr - d) along the axis; threshold=200-Amount). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Amount x Rotation. Textured synth. SHIPPING node (Movements/Smear).

PAEBlackHole (HBlackHole radial gravity lens: MIP pyramid of numLevels=max(1,round(log2(Amount/8))) radial warp nodes, each re-warping the original and composited via edge-falloff; per-level radius=Amount/(2/2^w21-1)). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Amount. Textured synth. SHIPPING node (Movements/Black_Hole).

PAERadialBlur (SPIN blur: FCP does polar-remap -> 1-D Gaussian along the ANGLE axis -> inverse-remap, amount'=Amount*1.5). Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Amount. Smooth synth (spin about centre). NOTE: engine's radialBlur('spin') rotates in SCREEN space (PHASE-2 TODO P2-RB1) — this node characterizes the screen-vs-polar-space gap.
nodes.characterized_reason: Engine implements FCP's polar-space spin pipeline (rect->polar, 1-D wrap-Gaussian on the angle axis, polar->rect; sigma=arcPx/6.0) and now reads the CORRECT Amount param (id=2, not id=1 -- the initial probe used id=1 which FCP ignored, a probe bug found+fixed this session). With id=2 the spin responds but carries a systematic signed bias (~-2 to -3 lvl, growing with Amount: 33.6 dB @amt3 -> 29.4 dB @amt8 -> 22.8 dB @amt30) -- the engine's angular kernel width vs FCP's differs, on top of the shared polar-resample discretization ceiling (~24 dB on all-edges content, documented in directional-blur.ts PHASE-2 CEILING NOTE). The Amount->arc scaling (FCP amount'=Amount*1.5) and the sigma constant need a clean spin-arc decode (a non-wrapping off-centre wedge probe -- the disc/spoke synths wrap +/-pi around the spin centre and can't measure the arc FWHM). Shipping users author Amount static so gate-inert. Small-amount regime {3,5,8} registered.

nodes.desc: PAEBevel FIXED+VERIFIED (2026-07-23): bevels the FULL-FRAME CANVAS RECTANGLE edges (NOT the alpha silhouette). Four flat-tinted plateau bands of width band_px=BevelWidth*0.28125*maxDim laid inward from the frame edges; tint=mix(pixel,T_edge,0.80*opacity) toward lightColor*lobe; NEAREST edge wins at corners; alpha unioned so the band fills the transparent border. Default-light lobes (headless-verified, Light Angle not probe-drivable): TOP=255 sides=135 bottom=114. Node-boundary full-frame PSNR over Bevel Width. SHIPPING (Stylized__Panels_Across). Was DIVERGED 9.5 dB with the old (wrong) alpha-gradient-normal model; now VERIFIED 47 dB.

PAEEarthquake at its node boundary, rendered at t=0 (frameScaled=trunc(secs*fps*1000)=0 for ANY fps, so the RNG seed is exactly seed+0x232323+0 and the time->frame host ambiguity vanishes). The per-frame shake pose is a DETERMINISTIC LCG+Bays-Durham-shuffle draw (a=4096,c=150889,m=714025,NTAB=101) recovered verbatim from the arm64 disasm; amplitudes rot=twist*0.1*(2r-1) rad, tx=hShake*25*(2r-1)px, ty=vShake*25*(2r-1)px about the epicenter pivot, layers averaged 1/L. RNG decode PROVEN pixel-exact: at t=0 hShake=8 shifts a disc 144.00px vs the RNG-predicted 143.84px (dy=0). ALL cases now VERIFIED >=40 dB after the pixel-center (texel-center +0.5) rotation-pivot fix: h8=46.6/v8=50.6/twist8=44.9/all5/seed7_all5 dB. The RNG (LCG+Bays-Durham), transform (rotation about epicenter + translation), AND resampler are all exact at the t=0 node boundary. Earlier twist8 38.6 dB was the missing texel-center convention (same fix family as Pixellate/Fisheye), NOT a decode gap. [2026-07-23: PROMOTED to VERIFIED — the pixel-center (texel-center) rotation fix (+0.5 pivot / −0.5 sample, same family as Pixellate/Fisheye) lifted the pure-rotation twist8 case 38.2→44.9 dB; full sweep worst_psnr=46.65 dB, all 5 cases ≥40. RNG+translation were already exact; the rotation resampler was the only gap.]

PAEUnderwater at its node boundary, rendered at t=0 (timeVal=Speed*secs=0, so the wave-field phases are the static seed phases and the render is deterministic/fps-independent). FULLY DECODED this session: (1) the exact wave-field RNG — a Numerical-Recipes LCG X<-(4096X+150889) mod 714025 with a Bays-Durham NTAB=101 shuffle, fill-seeded 0x23232323, shuffle iy seeded the CONSTANT 0x6f638 (buildFieldExact, bit-verified); (2) the HgcUnderwaterRefractV2 displacement = 10 independent weighted sines (NO cross-terms; verified by shader trace), each a per-octave BILINEAR-in-position phase ramp (corner-lerp of the M-projected endpoint pack) weighted by the octave amp vector; (3) the U,V mapping = an AFFINE (skewed) host pixel-transform M, JOINT-fit across Size=50 AND Size=100 (flow rms 0.49/0.61px, at the ~0.5px centroid-measurement floor). The old belief that the field needs an 'unrecoverable runtime noise texture' was DISPROVEN (RefractV2 samples the source exactly once via the refracted uv; FreqSynth is a separate/legacy path). Engine path behind FCT_UNDERWATER_EXACT (shipped apply() stays gate-safe passthrough while GUI-GT chain work is pending).
nodes.characterized_reason: DECODED + verified end-to-end at the node boundary (t=0): exact-RNG wave field + corner-lerp bilinear-phase-ramp displacement (10 weighted sines, no cross-terms) + affine host-M U,V. Node-boundary PSNR vs headless FCP: 30.9 dB (Size=50) / 29.8 dB (Size=100), up from the surrogate's 17.8-21.8 dB (+~12 dB) and the smooth-synth reads 32.2 dB. The residual to the 40 dB gate is NOT the displacement model — the flow-fit residual is at the ~0.5px centroid-measurement floor (rms 0.49/0.61px, spatial corr 0.90-0.97) and bicubic does not beat bilinear — it is the bilinear resample of the fine textured probe plus the sub-pixel displacement floor. The LAST decode step for the gate is pinning the affine U,V to the EXACT host pixel-transform M (sp+0x438..0x468 doubles) read from the binary rather than jointly fit. Same decoded-but-resample/measurement-ceiling family as spatial.PAEBloom (27 dB) and spatial.PAEZoomBlur (13.9 dB). Gate-inert: shipped apply() is the passthrough; exact path is env-gated.
